item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love reading SCPs with political and/or ideological connection.

Karl Marx is proud yet he is shocked that those followers are spiders.
The bourgeoisie will fear them. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God dammit. I just realized.

These are arachno-communists.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Precognitive pun!

item.title: I like this post
item.content:encoded:
This is a fun post to read. Not only its about communism something that I find funny due to people taking the mick out of it, also turn something as spiders something I fear forever, turning them to communist somehow make me less fearful of them as we can communicate with them.

Lastly I just want to hear those spiders speak in Russian.

item.title: SCP-1006-A
item.content:encoded: This is a fun and mysterious SCP and I do like it, however it might be good to designate the human corpes as scp-1006-A that's all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This put a huge grin on my face. They're like 1970s cartoon villains. Have a +1, this is lovely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have terrible aracnophobia, but after following a thread of references to this article, I ultimately had to upvote it for its sheer creativity and underlying horror. Excellent work.

I'll be feeling phantom spiders for the rest of the night.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the worst thing I've ever read and I love it. +1

item.title: Re: Thank yous
item.content:encoded: 1006, Communist China needs your help!
happy +300 to our benevolent leader Marxist spiders!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Communist spiders. Beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Touche

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who says they hate humans? They hate the bourgeoisie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a bit curious why this is safe? I mean so far they haven't actually tried to escape, but I feel like a growing colony of spiders that hate humans could be seriously dangerous if they put their minds to it. Otherwise like it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image in this article was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact anqxyr or a member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't like this. Not one bit. I was so ready to down vote this. Then I read that last line. I don't know what's going on here, but that last line somehow saved it. Well played.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of spiders that utilize Communism is quite fascinating, in my opinion. Keep up the good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just Putin in my opinion

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I live close to Big Bend National Park. Oh god. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's an extremely large value of "one" that you've got there.
In any case, TexasBigfoot last posted over a year ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Communist spiders! I love it!

One question: How do they make their signs? Do they work together to pick up a pen or a brush? Do they dip their little spider bodies in ink and then run up and down the board to make the message? What if they spelled out their messages using their webs, Charlotte-style? Inquiring minds want to know!

United forever in friendship and labor
Our great spider Union will 'ever endure…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To you it's a joke. To them it's "please stop poisoning our food".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was very fun to read. I almost thought the mosquito-spray bit was a bit too much, because it sounded like a joke, but… I don't know, that's probably a realistic request, and given everything else being great, I can certainly forgive it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with how this was handled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So a bunch of spiders ate a political radical's brain and gained his knowledge and opinions (but not his memories)? Or no, a SINGLE spider ate the guy's brain, and also consumed his intelligence and views? Something like that? That seems the most SCP-ish explanation to me…and it's GOOD.

item.title: Spider Society
item.content:encoded:
Spiders. All over. Did a search for spider and found a number of articles. Interesting to see them come up different in most articles.

This one I enjoyed, was fun to read through and would love to see it expanded upon, see the spiders start doing something more. Government over throw? War against humans? Human-spider fair trade discussions?

Anyways, thing that made my cringe was the final note about the dead body found. Thought it was going to be something creepy, something to hint at greater things. What it was made me feel like it was too on the nose about how this all came about.

item.title: Re: Thank yous
item.content:encoded:
It's all semantic nitpickery; my point was that Marxism was first devised in (and thus can be described as "of") the nineteenth century, whereas Nazism was first devised in (and thus is 'of') the twentieth.

As the old woman in East Berlin said to the American reporter after the Wall came down, "I have lived under Nazism, and I have lived under Communism, and the difference is: with Communism, the idea is nicer."

Communism is a lovely idea, but it requires "spherical workers of uniform density" — that is, it makes several idealized assumptions about human behavior, human nature, and human thought. If human nature was such that communism could actually work, we wouldn't need for it to be implemented, because that would be our natural state.

The reason that communism in the real world has only ever been applied by murderous tyrants is that, whenever anyone who's not a murderous tyrant tries to implement it, they only get so far before the population resists. And once the population is resisting, the leader can either stop (and thus not have communism), or brutally crush them. And any leader willing to brutally crush resistance to the implementation of communism is also willing to use brutality for all other purposes.

Communism doesn't cause murderous tyrants, it selects for them — a subtle distinction, and one which some would argue makes no difference.

That said, this is a significant derail. Whether what the spiders are doing really counts as "Marxist communism" is an interesting question; they think it does, but I don't know what a political scientist would say.

item.title: Re: Thank yous
item.content:encoded: I know this is a necropost but historical manipulation for political reasons annoys me a lot. Bear in mind how long the USSR and communist China were around compared to Nazi Germany. Also when it comes to the cold war both sides were as bad as each other the US just had third world dictators commit their atrocities with supplied weapons (eg. Chile, Guatemala, Venezuala, Nicragua, Cuba etc)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quick, he's about to Trotsky away

item.title: Re: Thank yous
item.content:encoded: (oh hey, I remember this, and I never did finish explaining what I meant: to wit, regardless of comparative body counts and second-, third-, and fourth-order effects, Marxism was an ideology of the nineteenth century, whereas Nazism was an ideology of the twentieth century.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:

i rokonuxa hereby announce that this comment was a simple joke.
It is and will never be a subject for serious discussion unless otherwise stated.
thank you for your time

item.content:encoded:
the cheers for Rumetzen

hip-hip hooray
hip-hip hooray
hip-hip hooray

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How about we don't?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
how about we get every character a companion?
these spiders for dimitri, the butterflys for kondraki, any frikkin demon-thing for clef, the broken god for gears, the sleep-replicating human for bright dont do that. xk is bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Spiders. It had to be spiders. +1 because this is going to effect my sleep schedule for the next week.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just unnerving beneath the funny enough to work really, really well as a mainlist article. As a dedicated arachnaphobe, I am both deeply amused and severely wigged out at the same time. Congratulations, author. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do like this one, but I'm not entirely on-board with how it uses the word "Occasional", it doesn't seem in line with what the tone should be for the page.

The surrounding area has a nauseating odor due to the presence of thousands of dead insects, individual members of SCP-1006, and an occasional dead bird and small animal

surrounded by dead insects and a variety of small shiny objects such as bottle caps and pebbles, along with the occasional pocket watch and bit of metal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let me Lennon you a hand with those puns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … and then you realize that "a request for less mosquito spraying in the surrounding area of the park" means "please stop poisoning our food".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spiders both horroify me and astound me. The idea that they'd be able to create their own society…

Well, I'll get the bug spray.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would give this an upvote, but it feels like it lacks a reaction, could maybe use a reference to a researcher theorizing why they would have such a specific form of government, or how they even know OF western media, being spiders confined to a single area.

This might just be me, but right now it feels more like an SCP that someone heard of rather than one The Foundation has contained and are actively researching.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They are about a match for each other , actually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvote for the Glorious Peoples Revolutionary Republic (of spiders).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the most adorable thing involving spiders ever. Charlotte's Web as written by George Orwell. Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Somehow this is the best and the worst thing I've read on here. Unlike some I love the idea and think it's awesome, but at the same time…it's so goddamn stupid, and yet I love it anyway.

This would work as a -J with a few tweaks, but it works main list, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congrats on the +100!
In Soviet Spiderweb, upvote give you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't "get" the body until I re-read it just now.

My understanding is now that he was their Lenin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just remember, the spiders are out there, and their arrival is inevitable: http://baen.com/SocialSpider.asp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Um, it's called marxism-leninism for a reason.
But well… i doubt this thread's purpose is to dispel ideological misconceptions.

That said this SCP is a perfect example of how execution matters. When I first heard of the concept , I reacted with "meh" and "WTF is this an SCP" precisely due to them existing in nature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe I'm using the wrong words for what I mean. Let me clarify in an overly verbose manner.
Many insects (and some spiders aparently) have naturally-occuring, cooperative, social (small "s") organizations for mutual benefit. Nature documentaries like to draw analogies between ant-hills/bee-hives and a Socialist (big "S") ideal society, as typified by the works of Lenin. It's such a common reference, that social insects=Socialist collective would be a cliche SCP before it was even posted.
But it is very uncommon for anyone to refer to social insects as Communist/Marxist, which is a distinct ideology from Socialism/Leninism despite their aparent similarities. Also, before your fascinating link, I had never heard of social (or Social) spiders at all.

What impressed me was the slight tweak on an otherwise born-trite concept which yielded a perfectly sound and subtly disturbing SCP. One that makes me giggle and worry at the same time.

…or maybe I should take some more cough syrup and go back to bed, I dunno.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mother Nature always steals the best ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
aaaaaaa!

*gasp*

AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um, eusocial spiders do exist IRL

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is goofy, yet disturbing, and it feels oddly…inevitable, I guess that's the word I want. "Socialist" ants and bees is such a common analogy that finding a Leninist hive wouldn't even feel like an anomalous object, much less an SCP. But Marxist arachnids are enough of a jar from the expected to really work.
I can't say that I find the colony scary, but I feel like I should, which oddly makes them creepier than if they were outright frightening. It's like the spiders are deliberately making themselves appear non-threatening and cartoonish. And if they are clever enough to pull off that ruse, what are they really hiding.

Well done, very well done, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you elaborate on why you feel that way?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts exactly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This should be a -J.

Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea of spiders forming a government triggers every single negative impulse my body is capable of. I have to go and do something else before I spew all over my keyboard.

I guess that's worth an upvote.
